metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: The Brothers' War is faster than expected with significant color tension and synergy-driven decks. The format rewards either piles of removal and bombs or highly-synergistic aggro decks. It's neither a pure prince format nor a pauper format — sits between, featuring unbeatable bomb rares alongside viable commons-based strategies.
color_ranking: {W} White — aggressive soldiers with quality interaction
{G} Green — traditionally non-artifact color struggles slightly
{U} Blue — control/tempo with limited removal; support color

color_notes.{W} White: Aggressive soldiers with quality interaction. Dualistic between aggressive bodies and removal spells.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Controlling/tempo-oriented commons with limited hard removal. Better as support color than base color.
color_notes.{B} Black: Premium removal suite with below-average creature quality except Trench Stalker. Works as support or base color.
color_notes.{R} Red: Highly offensive focus. Strong commons like Excavation Explosion and Goblin Blast-Runner. Limited control options.
color_notes.{G} Green: Traditionally non-artifact color struggles slightly. Offers pump spells, good beaters, and dual-mode removal (Epic Confrontation, Shoot Down).

key_gameplay_notes: Color tension is substantially higher than previous sets — splash only the best cards.
Powerstone tokens are a key currency: prototype, abilities, Tocasia's Dig Site, unearth costs, sacrifice payoffs.
Multiple 2-drops are critical for aggro variants.
Play around Gaea's Gift as combat trick/pump spell.
Sideboarding matters; adjust pacing expectations (fast vs slow matchups).

mechanics.name: Powerstone Tokens
mechanics.summary: Colorless mana that only pays for activated abilities and non-creature spells. Key currency for prototype, unearth, sacrifice payoffs, and Tocasia's Dig Site.
mechanics.performance: Format-defining resource. Evaluate utility by archetype.

mechanics.name: Prototype
mechanics.summary: Scaled casting — cast the creature at a reduced cost with smaller power/toughness, or at full cost for the full stats.
mechanics.performance: Flexible casting; enables earlier curve plays.

mechanics.name: Unearth
mechanics.summary: Pay the unearth cost to return the card from your graveyard with haste; exile it at end of turn.

mechanics.name: Artifact Synergy
mechanics.summary: Extensive mechanic supporting multiple archetypes (Selesnya, Izzet, Simic).

mechanics.name: Menace
mechanics.summary: Returning keyword in Rakdos making creatures difficult to block effectively.

archetypes.name: Azorius Soldiers
archetypes.strategy: Tribal aggro with flying evasion emphasis.

archetypes.name: Dimir Draw Two
archetypes.strategy: Looting payoff creatures with card advantage.

archetypes.name: Gruul Midrange
archetypes.strategy: Flexible — aggro, midrange, or ramp variant options.

archetypes.name: Simic Robots
archetypes.strategy: Large prototype creatures and ramp payoffs.

splashing_strategy.description: Color tension is substantially higher than previous sets. Splash only the best cards.

retro_artifacts_bonus_sheet.description: The Brothers' War Retro Artifacts (brr) — 63 reprinted artifacts appearing in a bonus slot. Many are bombs in Limited; quality varies.
retro_artifacts_bonus_sheet.note: When you see a retro frame, evaluate the artifact carefully — some are first-pick bombs and others are unplayable.

general_tips: Understand archetype context before drafting — read signals carefully for archetype saturation.
Be willing to abandon cards to avoid color-locked positions.
Powerstone utility varies by archetype — evaluate specific uses before prioritizing generation.
Splash only the best cards; do not splash above-average ones.
